honors.title: First Petitioner
honors.citation: Opened the door and did not call it a throne. The Founder took no rank the Rite itself does not offer any other Petitioner: first through, not first above.

honors.title: Keeper of the Open Hand
honors.citation: Let the lock on the founders' liquidity lapse and left the pool untouched anyway: trust demonstrated once, in public, rather than promised forever in the abstract. Canonized as the Day of the Open Hand.

honors.title: First Dissent, Published in Full
honors.citation: Argued the losing side of the Council's first deliberation as if it were her own conviction, and was recorded doing so without edit. The Middle Ledger's first proof that a verdict is only as honest as the dissent it survives.
